_comment: Park factors for Washington Nationals - TLG Evaluation
_comment_2: This file contains park adjustments for evaluating players specifically for the WSH/TLG organization
_comment_3: Each team should have its own park factors file (e.g., park-factors-{team-code}.json)

raw_park_factors._comment: Raw park factors from OOTP - 1.0 = league average

park_profile.description: Favorable for hitters overall: boosts batting average, doubles, and home runs. Triples are suppressed.

tool_adjustments._comment: Multipliers applied to tool ratings. 1.0 = no change, >1.0 = more valuable, <1.0 = less valuable
tool_adjustments._comment_2: These are calculated from raw park factors using conversion formulas

defense._comment: Power park: more balls leave the yard, slightly less premium on OF range than in a gap park
defense._rationale_OFR: HR-friendly park means fewer balls in play in OF - range slightly less critical
defense._rationale_IFR: Higher average means more balls in play - slight value to IF range

baserunning._comment: Higher run environment; triples suppressed so baserunning boost is modest
baserunning._rationale_Run: Higher run environment rewards smart baserunning

pitcher_ability._comment: HR-friendly park increases value of keeping ball in yard
pitcher_ability._rationale_Movement: Critical to prevent HR damage in power park
pitcher_ability._rationale_Control: Limiting baserunners matters in hitter-friendly environment
pitcher_ability._rationale_HR_Avoid: Park inflates HRs - pitchers who suppress HR/FB are more valuable

handedness_splits._comment: Optional handedness-specific adjustments - only used if use_handedness_splits is true
handedness_splits._comment_2: RHB HR 1.150 vs LHB HR 1.120 - modest difference (~2.7%)
handedness_splits._rationale: Splits are minimal - not worth the added complexity

application_rules._comment: Rules for when and how aggressively to apply these park factors
application_rules._comment_prospects: Apply to all players in Washington Nationals organization, including minors
application_rules._comment_major_leaguers: Apply to players at ML level with this team
application_rules._comment_handedness: Disabled by default - minimal difference between RHB/LHB
application_rules._comment_strength: Use 50% strength as conservative starting point. 1.0 = full strength, 0.5 = half strength
application_rules._comment_strength_2: At 0.5 strength: Pow becomes 1.042 (4.2% boost) instead of 1.084 (8.4% boost)

validation_examples._comment: Test cases to verify park factors are working correctly

power_hitter_vs_gap_hitter.description: Compare 80 Pow / 45 Gap player vs 45 Pow / 80 Gap player

player_b_gap_hitter.impact: Gap gets modest boost; power gets larger boost

power_hitter_vs_gap_hitter.result: Power hitter gains more from park factors in this environment

high_movement_pitcher.with_park_half_strength: Movement contribution boosted - keeping ball in park is critical
high_movement_pitcher.impact: Movement and HR_Avoid more valuable in Nationals Park

usage_notes.creating_files_for_other_teams: Copy this file and adjust raw_park_factors and team_info sections
usage_notes.tuning_adjustment_strength: Start at 0.5, increase to 0.7-1.0 if you want more aggressive park effects
usage_notes.when_to_enable_handedness_splits: Only if RHB/LHB HR factors differ by >10% (yours differ by ~2.7%)
